# Issue 058: JSDoc contains programming jargon — AminoAcid

**File:** `src/bio/AminoAcid.ts`

**Violation:** Constructor JSDoc uses programming parameter documentation style.

**Current JSDoc:**
```
A single amino acid, the building block of proteins.
Each amino acid has a characteristic atomic composition.
Natural amino acids in proteins are L-isomers.
```

**Constructor JSDoc:**
```
@param name The full name of the amino acid.
@param threeLetterCode The three-letter abbreviation.
@param oneLetterCode The one-letter abbreviation.
@param atomicComposition The stoichiometric composition of this amino acid.
```

**Problem:**
- Class JSDoc is chemically correct ✅
- But `@param` tags describe programming parameters rather than chemical properties
- "The full name", "The three-letter abbreviation" — these are nomenclature conventions, not chemical facts

**Expected:** The class-level JSDoc is good. The constructor `@param` descriptions should reference chemical nomenclature conventions (IUPAC name, standard three-letter and one-letter codes used in protein sequence notation).
